0.original: YJM lives in a new home. 2 00:00:03,921 --> 00:00:09,411 In every big decision, the mind battles with itself, to act or not to act, 3 00:00:09,521 --> 00:00:11,411 to do or not to do. 4 00:00:12,000 --> 00:00:16,771 Here's how one young fellow elected to act and reaped big rewards. 5 00:00:17,601 --> 00:00:21,970 J.M.'s situation is similar to that of a million other young men. 6 00:00:22,081 --> 00:00:27,731 He is in his twenties, has a wife and child, and still has only a modest income. 7 00:00:28,161 --> 00:00:31,330 Mr. and Mrs. J.M. lived in a small apartment. 8 00:00:31,441 --> 00:00:33,330 Both wanted a new home. 9 00:00:33,521 --> 00:00:39,330 They wanted the advantage of more space, cleaner surroundings, a place for the youngster to play, 10 00:00:39,361 --> 00:00:42,690 and a chance to build up equity in their own property. 11 00:00:43,121 --> 00:00:47,090 But there was a hitch to buying a new home, the down payment. 12 00:00:47,361 --> 00:00:53,651 One day, as J.M. was writing next month's rent check, he became very disgusted with himself. 13 00:00:54,001 --> 00:00:59,330 He observed that the rent payment was as much as monthly payments on a new home. 14 00:00:59,921 --> 00:01:05,090 J.M. called his wife and said, How would you like to buy a new home next week? 15 00:01:05,361 --> 00:01:06,690 What's got into you? 16 00:01:06,721 --> 00:01:07,491 she asked. 17 00:01:07,681 --> 00:01:09,011 Why make jokes? 18 00:01:09,041 --> 00:01:10,210 You know we can't. 19 00:01:10,321 --> 00:01:13,171 We haven't even got the money for the down payment. 20 00:01:13,521 --> 00:01:15,570 but J.M. was determined. 21 00:01:15,841 --> 00:01:21,251 There are hundreds of thousands of couples like us who are going to buy a new home someday, 22 00:01:21,361 --> 00:01:23,570 but only about half of them ever do. 23 00:01:23,841 --> 00:01:26,131 Something always comes up to stop them. 24 00:01:26,401 --> 00:01:28,210 We're going to buy a home. 25 00:01:28,481 --> 00:01:32,450 I don't know yet how we'll raise the down payment, but we will. 26 00:01:33,121 --> 00:01:41,491 Well, the next week, they found a house they both liked, quite unpretentious but nice, for $1,200 down. 27 00:01:41,841 --> 00:01:46,690 Now, the obstacle was to find a way to raise $1,200. 28 00:01:46,881 --> 00:01:55,330 J.M. knew he couldn't borrow it through the usual channels, for this would encumber his credit so that he couldn't get a mortgage for the sale price. 29 00:01:55,921 --> 00:01:58,531 Where there's a will, there's always a way. 30 00:01:58,641 --> 00:02:01,411 Suddenly, J.M. got a brainstorm. 31 00:02:01,681 --> 00:02:07,810 Why not contact the builder and work out a private loan arrangement for $1,200? 32 00:02:07,921 --> 00:02:09,731 This J.M. did. 33 00:02:10,081 --> 00:02:15,011 At first, the builder was cold to the idea, but J.M. persisted. 34 00:02:15,201 --> 00:02:15,665 Finally, it was agreed.
0.b1: J.M. lives in a new home.
0.ai_notes:
'battles with itself': refers to the internal struggle or hesitation when making a difficult choice.
'young fellow': a friendly or informal way to refer to a young man.
'reaped big rewards': to gain significant benefits or success as a result of one's actions.
'modest income': earning a salary that is enough for basic needs but not very high.
'equity': the value of a property that the owner truly owns after subtracting any mortgage debt.
'hitch': a minor problem or unexpected difficulty that causes a delay.
'down payment': an initial payment made when buying something expensive on credit, like a house.
'disgusted with himself': feeling very unhappy or annoyed with one's own situation or behavior.
'What's got into you?': an idiom used to ask why someone is acting strangely or unexpectedly.
'comes up': refers to an event or problem that happens suddenly and unexpectedly.
'unpretentious': simple, modest, and not trying to look more expensive or important than it really is.
'encumber': to burden or restrict someone, making it difficult for them to do something else.
'Where there's a will, there's a way': a famous proverb meaning that if you are determined enough, you can find a way to achieve your goal.
'brainstorm': a sudden, clever idea or a moment of creative thinking.
'cold to the idea': to show no interest or enthusiasm for a suggestion.
0.remarks:
'battles with itself': 指在做困难选择时的内心挣扎或犹豫。
'young fellow': 对年轻男性的友好或非正式称呼。
'reaped big rewards': 因某人的行为而获得巨大的利益或成功。
'modest income': 收入仅够基本需求，不算很高。
'equity': 房产扣除抵押贷款后，业主真正拥有的价值。
'hitch': 导致延误的小问题或意外困难。
'down payment': 购买昂贵物品（如房产）时支付的首付款。
'disgusted with himself': 对自己的处境或行为感到非常不快或厌烦。
'What's got into you?': 用于询问某人为何表现得奇怪或反常的习语。
'comes up': 指突然且意外发生的事件或问题。
'unpretentious': 朴实、谦逊，不刻意显得昂贵或重要。
'encumber': 给某人造成负担或限制，使其难以做其他事。
'Where there's a will, there's a way': 一句名言，意为只要有足够的决心，就能找到实现目标的方法。
'brainstorm': 突然产生的聪明点子或创造性思维时刻。
'cold to the idea': 对某项建议表现出没有兴趣或热情。
